---
title: "Jupyter Notebook to Markdown conversion"
author: "Sanne Govaert"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

## 1. Setup

### Install Quarto

Install Quarto via Homebrew (macOS):

```bash
brew install --cask quarto
```

### Download the file

Download the raw file from
https://github.com/RYCKEWAERT/deepmaxent/blob/main/tutorial_deepmaxent.ipynb

Save it in `/src/external/raw/deepmaxent`.


## 2. Create documentation

### Render the notebook to Markdown

In terminal, set the working directory to the folder containing the notebook 
(`/src/external/raw/deepmaxent`) and render it to GitHub-Flavoured
Markdown (`.gfm`). The rendered `.md` file is written to the interim directory.

```bash
cd src/external/raw/deepmaxent

quarto render deepmaxent.ipynb \
  --to gfm \
  --output-dir ../../../external/interim/deepmaxent
```

### Move figures to the public directory

Quarto places figure assets in a `<notebook-name>-files/` folder alongside
the rendered Markdown. Move this folder to the correct location under
`public/` so the documentation site can serve the images.

First, move to the root of the project folder (`documentation`). Then run:

```bash
mkdir -p public/software/deepmaxent

rm -rf public/software/deepmaxent/deepmaxent_files

mv src/external/interim/deepmaxent/deepmaxent_files \
   public/software/deepmaxent/
```

### Run `rmd_to_md.Rmd`

Use b3doc to add metadata and change the path to the figures.


## 3. Clean markdown

- Add the source to the frontmatter
- Remove the table of contents
- Clean the H2 titles (##)
